nodes.name: Income Tax Group
nodes.description: The Income Tax Group is a government organization responsible for income tax collection and administration in Pakistan.<SEP>The Income Tax Group, now renamed as Inland Revenue Service, is a department within the government.<SEP>Income Tax Group is an occupational group within the government service, renamed as Inland Revenue Service.<SEP>The Income Tax Group is an occupational group under the Ministry of Finance.<SEP>Income Tax Group, now part of the Inland Revenue Service, is an occupational group.<SEP>Income Tax Group is a government organization dealing with income tax.

nodes.name: Military Lands And Cantonment Group
nodes.description: Military Lands and Cantonment Group is an occupational group.

nodes.name: Information Group
nodes.description:
The Information Group is a multifaceted entity that encompasses various departments and organizations, primarily under the umbrella of government services related to information and media. It is recognized as an occupational group within the government service, comprising a range of roles and responsibilities centered around information dissemination and management. The Information Group operates under the aegis of the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, although it is also associated with the Ministry of Information and Media Development in certain contexts.

As a government service, the Information Group is tasked with overseeing information functions, which may include media development, public relations, and the dissemination of governmental information to the public. It is also notable for being one of the specified ministries or divisions where armed forces officers can be re-employed, highlighting its significance within the government's organizational structure. The Information Group's role is not limited to a single ministry or department; it is an integral part of the government's apparatus for managing and regulating information and media-related activities, particularly in countries like Pakistan.

The Information Group's responsibilities and organizational structure may vary, reflecting the diversity of its roles and the different governmental contexts in which it operates. Despite these variations, its core function as a hub for information and media activities remains consistent, underscoring its importance in the governance and public information sectors. As an occupational group, it provides a framework for careers and professional development in fields related to information, media, and public service, further emphasizing its significance within the broader spectrum of government operations and public administration.

nodes.name: Postal Group
nodes.description:
The Postal Group is an occupational group comprising various posts within the government service, specifically responsible for postal functions. It operates under the Ministry of Communications and is a part of the Pakistan Post Office Department. The Postal Group is a government organization that provides essential postal services, playing a vital role in the country's communication infrastructure.

As a government department or agency, the Postal Group is dedicated to delivering postal services to the public. Its responsibilities include managing and maintaining the postal system, ensuring efficient and reliable delivery of mail and packages. The Postal Group's role is fundamental to the country's communication network, facilitating communication and exchange between individuals, businesses, and organizations.

The descriptions of the Postal Group highlight its dual nature as both an occupational group and a government organization. It is an entity that comprises various occupational posts, while also being a distinct government department or agency responsible for a specific set of functions. The Postal Group's structure and operations are designed to support the country's postal services, making it an essential component of the government's service delivery framework.

nodes.name: District Management Group
nodes.description: The District Management Group is another name for the Pakistan Administrative Service, which succeeded the Civil Service of Pakistan.<SEP>The District Management Group was renamed as the Pakistan Administrative Service.<SEP>District Management Group is an occupational group within the government service, renamed as Pakistan Administrative Service.<SEP>District Management Group, now renamed as Pakistan Administrative Service, is an occupational group.<SEP>District Management Group is a government organization dealing with district management.

nodes.name: Office Management Group
nodes.description:
The Office Management Group is an occupational group that comprises ministerial posts and section officers in the Federal Secretariat. It is a government service responsible for office management functions, and its primary role is to manage administrative tasks. The Office Management Group is a component of the Federal Government, and it is also a department responsible for overseeing office administration and management.

The Office Management Group is also known to be a specialized group that deals with office management and administration. It is involved in the management of office affairs and regulations, and it provides personnel for administrative tasks. In some contexts, the Office Management Group is referred to as a service group or cadre, with its administrative control held by the Establishment Division. The group is also handled by the Establishment Division, which highlights its importance within the government's organizational structure.

In Pakistan, the Office Management Group is a government department that handles administrative tasks, and it is one of the specified ministries/divisions/services groups for re-employment of armed forces officers. The group is also equivalent to the Central Secretariat Service, which provides personnel for administrative tasks. Overall, the Office Management Group plays a crucial role in managing office operations, overseeing staff, and ensuring the smooth functioning of government offices.

The Office Management Group is a distinct entity within the government, and it is separate from other groups, such as the Secretariat Group, to which officers can be promoted from the Office Management Group. The group's responsibilities and functions highlight its significance within the government's organizational structure, and its role in managing administrative tasks is essential to the effective functioning of government offices.

nodes.name: Inland Revenue Service
nodes.description:
The Inland Revenue Service is a government organization responsible for inland revenue collection and tax administration in Pakistan. It comprises all posts in the Income Tax Department and functions under the Federal Board of Revenue. The service is a new occupational service created for tax administration, including income tax, sales tax, and federal excise. As a department within the government, the Inland Revenue Service is responsible for tax collection functions, previously known as the Income Tax Group.

The Inland Revenue Service operates under the Ministry of Finance and is responsible for income tax, as well as other tax administration tasks. It is an occupational group that has undergone a name change from Income Tax Group to its current designation. The service plays a crucial role in tax collection and revenue management in Pakistan, enforcing tax laws and ensuring compliance with tax regulations. As a government agency, the Inland Revenue Service is responsible for collecting taxes and managing revenue, working to achieve the government's fiscal objectives.

The various roles and responsibilities of the Inland Revenue Service underscore its importance in Pakistan's tax administration framework. With its focus on income tax, sales tax, and federal excise, the service works to promote voluntary compliance and prevent tax evasion. Through its efforts, the Inland Revenue Service contributes to the government's revenue collection goals, supporting the development of the country's economy and public services. Overall, the Inland Revenue Service is a vital component of Pakistan's tax administration system, working to ensure the effective collection and management of inland revenue.

nodes.name: Pakistan Administrative Service
nodes.description:
The Pakistan Administrative Service is a civil service organization responsible for administering the country's federal and provincial bureaucracies. It is a part of the Civil Service of Pakistan, with officers appointed through direct recruitment or on an ad hoc basis from provincial civil services. The service is also known as the successor to the Civil Service of Pakistan, previously referred to as the District Management Group. The Pakistan Administrative Service fills certain posts in connection with the affairs of the provinces and is responsible for administrative affairs in Pakistan.

The service has various grades and promotions based on seniority and performance, and officers of the Military Lands and Cantonments Group can be eligible for induction into the Pakistan Administrative Service. It is one of the occupational groups and services that candidates can apply for, and it conducts competitive examinations for induction. The Pakistan Administrative Service is a government organization responsible for administrative roles, public services, and administrative matters in Pakistan. It is handled by the Establishment Division, which holds administrative control over the service.

The Pakistan Administrative Service is one of the All Pakistan Services, with sanctioned strength in BS 17 to 22 in the Federal Government and provincial governments. It is a civil service cadre responsible for the administration of the government, with officers inducted from various sources, including the armed forces. The service deals with administration and management of various government departments and agencies, and it is a department or organization within the government. The Pakistan Administrative Service is a branch of the government, responsible for administrative tasks and services, and it is a government service under the Establishment Division. Overall, the Pakistan Administrative Service plays a crucial role in the administration of Pakistan, and its officers are responsible for managing the federal government and provincial bureaucracies.

nodes.name: Office Memoranda
nodes.description: Office Memoranda are documents issued by government divisions to provide instructions and guidance.<SEP>Office Memoranda are documents that contain guidelines and regulations for various occupational groups.<SEP>Office Memoranda are official documents issued by Estt. Div. to convey instructions and guidelines.

nodes.name: Pakistan Customs Service
nodes.description:
The Pakistan Customs Service is a government organization responsible for customs and excise matters in Pakistan. It is a group comprising all posts in the Customs and Central Excise Department, under the overall administration of the Federal Board of Revenue. The service is responsible for customs and excise operations, including enforcing customs regulations and providing customs services. Previously known as the Customs and Excise Group, the Pakistan Customs Service is now a separate entity from the Inland Revenue Service.

The Pakistan Customs Service operates under the Ministry of Finance and is one of the occupational groups and services that candidates can apply for. As a law enforcement agency, it deals with customs operations and is responsible for enforcing customs regulations. The service is a department within the government, and its primary functions include customs functions, customs and excise operations, and providing customs services. The Pakistan Customs Service plays a crucial role in the country's economy, and its responsibilities include administering customs and excise laws, collecting revenue, and facilitating international trade.

The Pakistan Customs Service is a vital organization in Pakistan, responsible for ensuring the smooth operation of customs and excise matters. Its role is multifaceted, and it works to prevent smuggling, enforce customs regulations, and provide customs services to the public. With its rich history and evolution from the Customs and Excise Group, the Pakistan Customs Service continues to play a significant role in the country's economic development and growth. As a government organization, it is committed to providing efficient and effective customs services, facilitating trade, and contributing to the nation's revenue.

nodes.name: Ministry Of Law
nodes.description: Ministry Of Law is a government department that provides legal advice and consultation.<SEP>The Ministry of Law is a government agency that provided consultation regarding the interpretation of rules applicable to autonomous bodies.<SEP>The Ministry of Law is a government department that provides legal advice and interpretation of rules and regulations.<SEP>The Ministry of Law is a government department that participates in the Anti-Corruption Council.<SEP>Ministry of Law is a government department that provides legal advice and guidance to other government departments.

nodes.name: Appellate Authority
nodes.description: The Appellate Authority is a body that hears appeals related to disciplinary actions against government servants.<SEP>The Appellate Authority is a body or individual responsible for reviewing and deciding on appeals.

nodes.name: Natural Justice
nodes.description: Natural Justice refers to the principle of fairness and due process in decision-making, particularly in disciplinary actions.

nodes.name: Disciplinary Action
nodes.description: Disciplinary action refers to the measures taken against a re-employed officer for misconduct or poor performance.<SEP>Disciplinary action refers to the measures taken against a government servant for misconduct or unauthorized absence.<SEP>Disciplinary action refers to the measures taken against civil servants for misconduct or inefficiency.<SEP>Disciplinary action refers to the measures taken against a government servant for misconduct or wrongdoing.<SEP>Disciplinary action refers to the procedures and penalties imposed on government servants for misconduct or other offenses.

nodes.description: Rule 7 of the Civil Servants (Efficiency & Discipline) Rules, 2020, pertains to the procedure for disciplinary actions.<SEP>Rule 7 of the Civil Servants (Efficiency and Discipline) Rules, 2020, pertains to the provision of information and opportunity for the accused to show cause against proposed actions.

nodes.name: NGOs
nodes.description: NGOs (Non-Governmental Organizations) are private organizations that government servants may be allowed to work with on leave.<SEP>NGOs, or non-governmental organizations, are independent entities that may employ government servants or armed forces officers.

nodes.name: Foreign Governments
nodes.description: Foreign governments are governments of other countries that may employ government servants on leave.<SEP>Foreign Governments are entities that employ or collaborate with government officers in foreign countries.<SEP>Foreign Governments are external governments that collaborate with the Pakistani government.<SEP>Foreign governments are entities where government servants may be deputed to work.<SEP>Foreign governments are national governments of other countries, potentially involved in the employment or interaction with government servants or armed forces officers.<SEP>Foreign governments employ ex-government servants under specific terms and conditions, with regulations governing such employment.

nodes.name: International Organizations
nodes.description:
International Organizations are entities that operate globally, transcending national borders. They may employ government servants on leave, offer deputation opportunities, or hire functionaries from multiple countries. These organizations can include international governmental organizations, non-governmental organizations, and companies with global operations. Examples of International Organizations include the World Bank and the International Monetary Fund.

International Organizations are involved in various global activities and cooperation, providing a platform for collaboration among nations. Government servants may be deputed to work with these organizations, and they may also employ armed forces officers on the basis of leave or other arrangements. The terms and conditions of employment with International Organizations can vary, and they often require adherence to specific rules and regulations. Additionally, these organizations may offer benefits to their employees, whether they are government servants on deputation or regular employees.

The scope of International Organizations is broad, encompassing a wide range of activities and entities. They may hire functionaries from multiple countries, and Pakistani officers, among others, may be posted on deputation to work with these organizations. Overall, International Organizations play a significant role in global cooperation and activities, providing opportunities for collaboration and employment across national borders.

nodes.name: Government Orders
nodes.description: Government orders are official instructions or decisions made by the government, which may be related to service matters or financial regulations.<SEP>Government orders are official directives or instructions issued by the government, potentially related to the employment or re-employment of government servants or armed forces officers.

nodes.name: Re-Employment
nodes.description: Re-employment refers to the process of hiring back retired government servants, with specific rules and regulations governing this process.<SEP>Re-employment refers to the event of a retired government servant being hired again, which affects their eligibility for traveling allowance.<SEP>Re-Employment refers to the hiring of retired civil servants in government departments, autonomous/semi-autonomous organizations, or private companies.<SEP>Re-employment refers to the practice of hiring back government servants after they have retired or reached the age of superannuation.<SEP>Re-employment refers to the process of hiring or re-hiring individuals, potentially including government servants or armed forces officers, into new or previous positions.<SEP>Re-employment is the process of hiring a retired government servant back into a civil post.

nodes.name: Defence Services Officers Selection Board
nodes.description:
The Defence Services Officers Selection Board is a governing body responsible for evaluating and selecting candidates for various positions. The Board is involved in the selection of officers for the armed forces, as well as the re-employment of retired armed forces officers in civil services. This includes recommending retired officers of the Armed Forces of Pakistan for positions such as Deputy Secretary (BS-19). The Defence Services Officers Selection Board also plays a role in the resignation process of contract officers and is responsible for processing cases of retired officers for appointment in civil posts.

The Board's responsibilities extend to selecting officers for induction or re-employment in civil posts, and it is responsible for selecting and employing retired armed forces officers in civil posts. The Defence Services Officers Selection Board is also involved in the selection and allocation of armed forces officers for induction into civil services, although it is noted that this responsibility may have been previously held. The Board operates as a committee responsible for selecting defence services officers for various roles, including re-employment in specified service groups or ministries.

In summary, the Defence Services Officers Selection Board is a crucial entity responsible for the selection and re-employment of officers in various capacities, including the armed forces and civil services. Its responsibilities encompass a wide range of activities, from evaluating candidates to processing cases for appointment in civil posts. The Board's role is essential in ensuring the effective placement and re-employment of defence services officers, and its functions are critical to the operation of the armed forces and civil services.

nodes.name: Leave Salary
nodes.description: Leave Salary is the salary paid to civil servants during their leave period.<SEP>Leave salary refers to the payment made to government servants during their leave period.<SEP>Leave Salary refers to the payment made to a government servant during their leave period.<SEP>Leave Salary is a benefit paid to civil servants who proceed on leave preparatory to retirement.<SEP>Leave salary refers to the compensation or pay provided to individuals while they are on leave, potentially applicable to government servants.

nodes.name: Pension Contribution
nodes.description: Pension contribution is the amount paid by government servants towards their pension fund.<SEP>Pension Contribution is a payment made by or on behalf of a government servant towards their pension.<SEP>Pension contribution refers to the funds or amounts contributed towards an individual's pension, potentially applicable to government servants.

nodes.name: Private Employment
nodes.description: Private employment refers to jobs or positions within the private sector, potentially available to government servants or armed forces officers.

nodes.name: Welfare And Rehabilitation Directorate
nodes.description: Welfare and Rehabilitation Directorate is an organization responsible for issuing certificates for ex-servicemen employment.

nodes.name: Armed Forces Personnel
nodes.description: Armed Forces Personnel are members of the armed forces who can apply for age-relaxation, subject to production of departmental permission certificate.<SEP>Armed Forces Personnel are individuals who have served in the armed forces and are eligible for re-employment.

nodes.name: Service Chief
nodes.description: The Service Chief is a high-ranking officer in the armed forces who allocates officers to occupational groups.

nodes.name: Class IV Posts
nodes.description: Class IV Posts refer to a category of jobs in the government sector.

nodes.name: Combined Competitive Examination
nodes.description: Combined Competitive Examination is an event conducted by the FPSC for direct recruitment.

nodes.name: Additional Secretary-II
nodes.description: The Additional Secretary-II is a government official responsible for assisting the Secretary in Establishment Division.

nodes.name: Office Memorandum
nodes.description:
The Office Memorandum is a document issued by various government departments, including the Establishment Division and the Finance Division, for the purpose of regulating employment policies, providing guidelines and instructions, and communicating regulations and policies. It is used for official communication and policy implementation within government organizations, aiming to clarify or implement policies, outline policies and procedures, and provide guidance or instruction.

The Office Memorandum contains guidelines and rules for promotions and service counting in government jobs, and it outlines policies and decisions regarding government service. It is a formal document that serves as a means of issuing policies, instructions, and decisions within a government organization. The content of an Office Memorandum can vary, but it is generally used to address matters such as deputation, civil servant employment, and benefits.

While there are slight variations in the descriptions provided, with some attributing the issuance of Office Memorandums to the Establishment Division and others to the Finance Division, it appears that both divisions, as well as other government departments, can issue these documents. The Office Memorandum is a critical tool for government organizations, enabling them to communicate policies, regulations, and decisions in a formal and official manner. Overall, the Office Memorandum plays a significant role in the administration and management of government services, ensuring that policies and procedures are clearly communicated and implemented.

nodes.name: Administrative Division
nodes.description: An Administrative Division is a government department responsible for overseeing a particular sector or industry.

nodes.name: Autonomous Body
nodes.description:
An Autonomous Body is an independent organization that operates under its own authority, often established by the government to perform specific tasks. It has its own governing structure and operations, which may include employing individuals, as noted in the employment record categories, and can also receive government servants on deputation. While Autonomous Bodies are independent, they may still be subject to oversight by the government, whether federal or provincial, and are sometimes responsible for overseeing a particular sector or industry.

The level of independence and the nature of the relationship with the government can vary among Autonomous Bodies. Some operate under the guidance of the government, while others have more autonomy in their operations. They may be established by law, which defines their structure and responsibilities. In some cases, Autonomous Bodies can borrow government servants on deputation and are subject to re-employment policies when it comes to government servants.

Despite the variations in their establishment and operations, Autonomous Bodies share the characteristic of being independent organizations that work to achieve specific goals, often in conjunction with the government. Their ability to operate independently allows them to focus on their designated tasks, contributing to the overall functioning of the government and the sectors they oversee. The exact nature and scope of an Autonomous Body's independence and its relationship with the government can depend on its specific mandate and the legal framework under which it was established.

nodes.name: Foreign Service of Pakistan
nodes.description:
The Foreign Service of Pakistan is a government service responsible for foreign affairs functions, including diplomatic relations and international affairs. It is also known as the Foreign Affairs Group, as per the Establishment Division's Notification No. SRO 936(1)/83. This service operates under the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and is responsible for representing Pakistan abroad. The Foreign Service of Pakistan is considered an occupational group within the government service, and it is also a civil service cadre responsible for diplomatic and foreign affairs.

The service plays a crucial role in maintaining and promoting Pakistan's international relations, and it is the country's diplomatic service. The Foreign Service of Pakistan is also an avenue for the induction of armed forces officers into the civil service, providing an opportunity for them to contribute to the country's foreign affairs. As a government agency, the Foreign Service of Pakistan works to advance Pakistan's interests abroad and to promote cooperation with other countries.

In summary, the Foreign Service of Pakistan is a vital component of the country's government, responsible for managing its international relations and promoting its interests abroad. With its role in diplomatic relations, foreign affairs, and international cooperation, the Foreign Service of Pakistan is a key institution in Pakistan's engagement with the global community.

nodes.name: Police Service of Pakistan
nodes.description:
The Police Service of Pakistan is a government service responsible for law enforcement functions, maintaining law and order, and enforcing laws in the country. It is a law enforcement agency and a civil service cadre that serves as an occupational group for the induction of armed forces officers. The Police Service of Pakistan is part of the Central Services and is handled by the Establishment Division, which holds its administrative control. As a service group, it has a sanctioned strength in BS 17 to 22 in the Federal Government and provincial governments.

The Police Service of Pakistan is responsible for maintaining order and security in the country, and it is the country's primary law enforcement service. Its key functions include maintaining law and order, enforcing laws, and ensuring the safety and security of citizens. The service is an integral part of the government's efforts to maintain peace and stability in Pakistan. With its role in law enforcement and maintenance of order, the Police Service of Pakistan plays a critical role in the country's governance and public safety.

The Police Service of Pakistan operates under the administrative control of the Establishment Division, which oversees its operations and ensures that it functions effectively in maintaining law and order. As a government service, the Police Service of Pakistan is committed to upholding the law and protecting the rights of citizens. Its responsibilities are multifaceted, ranging from maintaining order and security to enforcing laws and regulations. The Police Service of Pakistan is a vital institution in Pakistan, and its role in maintaining public safety and order is essential to the country's well-being and development.

nodes.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
nodes.description:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs is a government ministry responsible for overseeing foreign affairs, international relations, and diplomacy. It is the administrative controller of the Foreign Affairs Group and is responsible for interactions with foreign missions and governments. The ministry plays a crucial role in managing the country's relationships with other nations and international organizations.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs is also involved in issuing circulars and guidelines related to foreign affairs, such as the employment of ex-government servants by UN agencies. As a government department, it is responsible for overseeing and coordinating the country's foreign policy, including diplomatic relations, trade agreements, and international cooperation. The ministry's responsibilities encompass a wide range of activities, from promoting the country's interests abroad to providing assistance to citizens traveling or living overseas.

Overall, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs is a key government entity that works to promote and protect the country's interests internationally, while also fostering cooperation and understanding with other nations. Through its various activities and initiatives, the ministry contributes to the development of a stable and peaceful international environment, and plays a vital role in shaping the country's foreign policy and international relations.

nodes.name: Grade 17
nodes.description:
Grade 17 is a rank in the civil service, characterized by a specific level of seniority and hierarchy. It is a level where officers are filled through a combination of competitive examination and promotion, with 75% of posts typically filled through examination and 25% through promotion. Within the government service hierarchy, Grade 17 is considered a higher grade, particularly in the Economists and Planners Group, where specific requirements and promotion procedures apply. It is also notable as an entry-level position in the Pakistan Administrative Service, indicating its significance as a starting point for careers in public administration.

Grade 17 is further defined by its presence in various government departments, including the Inland Revenue Service, where officers are selected through competitive examination and promotion. The grade is subject to specific regulations and requirements, including the need for approval from high-ranking officials for appointments and re-appointments. This level of posts in the government service is indicative of a structured system where progression and advancement are based on merit, experience, and adherence to established protocols. Overall, Grade 17 represents a crucial rung in the ladder of government service, reflecting both the competitive nature of entry into the civil service and the hierarchical structure that governs career progression within it.

nodes.name: Grade 18
nodes.description:
Grade 18 is a senior position within various government service hierarchies. It is a level where promotions are based on length of service and other criteria, particularly within the Inland Revenue Service. In the government hierarchy, Grade 18 is a service grade that requires a minimum of 5 years of service in Grade 17. However, the requirements can vary depending on the specific service or group, such as the Pakistan Administrative Service, which demands a minimum of 12 years of service in Grade 17 or 18.

Grade 18 is also recognized as a level of seniority in the All-Pakistan Services and the Economists and Planners Group, where seniority is determined by the regular appointment date. Furthermore, it is a notable grade in the Office Management Group and the Secretariat Group, where officers can be promoted to the post of Deputy Secretary. Across these different groups and services, Grade 18 signifies a higher level of responsibility and is a position that officers can be inducted into after meeting the specified criteria.

Despite the variations in requirements and responsibilities, Grade 18 is consistently regarded as a higher grade in the government service hierarchy, reflecting a significant level of experience and seniority. The specific details and criteria for achieving Grade 18 may differ, but its importance and standing within the civil service are universally acknowledged. Overall, Grade 18 represents a distinguished level of service and expertise, underscoring the progression and advancement opportunities available within the government's administrative structures.

nodes.name: Estt. Div.
nodes.description:
The Establishment Division (Estt. Div.) is a government department responsible for overseeing various administrative matters. Estt. Div. is known to issue a wide range of official documents, including notifications, office memorandums, and orders. These documents often pertain to personnel matters, such as the verification of educational certificates, deputation, promotion, and the conduct of government servants and pensioners.

The division plays a crucial role in the federal government, providing guidelines and regulations for autonomous bodies and issuing rules and regulations related to various aspects of government administration. Estt. Div. is also responsible for issuing directives and instructions to Ministries and Divisions, ensuring that all government entities operate in accordance with established policies and procedures.

In its capacity as a government department, Estt. Div. has issued numerous official memoranda, policies, and notifications, which are essential for the smooth functioning of government operations. The division's responsibilities also include overseeing administrative matters, such as the issuance of office memorandums and guidelines for deputation and promotion. Overall, the Establishment Division (Estt. Div.) is a vital component of the federal government, responsible for ensuring that all administrative matters are handled efficiently and effectively.

nodes.description: Special Professional Pay Scale is a pay scale used for hiring contractual appointments in Ministries/Divisions.<SEP>The Special Professional Pay Scale is a pay package introduced for hiring Special Professional Experts, with three tiers: SPPS-I, SPPS-II, and SPPS-III.<SEP>The Special Professional Pay Scale is a pay scale for contractual appointments in Ministries/Divisions.

nodes.name: Special Selection Board
nodes.description:
The Special Selection Board is a government committee responsible for overseeing various personnel matters related to government servants. It is constituted for the selection of Special Professional Experts, with varying compositions for different professional pay scales. The board is also responsible for selecting candidates for contractual appointments in the Special Professional Pay Scale. 

One of the primary functions of the Special Selection Board is to clear the employment of government servants with international organizations. It oversees the selection of government servants for deputation, including making the final selection of officers for posting abroad, which involves conducting interviews. The board reviews and approves the selection of candidates for appointment in Pakistan Missions Abroad and reviews nominations for deputation to foreign service.

The Special Selection Board plays a crucial role in deciding the postings of non-Foreign Service officers in Pakistan's missions abroad and the deputation of government servants to international agencies. It is also responsible for making decisions regarding the extension of deputation of government servants and decides on extensions and replacements of officers on deputation. Additionally, the board simplifies procedures for consultancy assignments and considers requests for relaxation of rules. 

The Special Selection Board recommends civil servants for deputation and is involved in making decisions on appointments, promotions, and other personnel matters related to government servants. It is a key body in the selection and deployment of government servants to various positions, both within the country and abroad. The board's roles and responsibilities highlight its significance in the management of human resources within the government sector.

nodes.name: Additional Secretary
nodes.description:
The Additional Secretary is a high-ranking government officer responsible for various administrative functions in the government hierarchy. This position can be filled by selection from amongst Joint Secretaries and other eligible officers. The Additional Secretary is an official who assists the Secretary and may sign proposals in their absence. They serve as a member of the Confirmation Committee and head in-house committees for scrutinizing applicant papers for Special Professional Expert positions.

In the Establishment Division, the Additional Secretary chairs the monitoring committee, playing a crucial role in the administrative process. As a high-ranking government official, they assist in the administration of a ministry or division, undertaking key responsibilities to ensure the smooth functioning of the government. The Additional Secretary's role is multifaceted, involving administrative, monitoring, and committee responsibilities, making them a vital part of the government's organizational structure.

The Additional Secretary's position is significant in the government's decision-making process, and their duties may vary depending on the specific ministry or division they are assigned to. Overall, the Additional Secretary is a key figure in the government's administrative framework, working closely with other officials to ensure the effective implementation of policies and programs. Their role is essential to the functioning of the government, and their responsibilities reflect the importance of their position in the government hierarchy.

nodes.id: Civil Servants Appointment Promotion And Transfer Rules
nodes.name: Civil Servants Appointment Promotion And Transfer Rules
nodes.description:
The Civil Servants Appointment, Promotion, and Transfer Rules, 1973, are guidelines that govern the appointment, promotion, and transfer of civil servants in the government. These rules outline the procedures for appointing, promoting, and transferring civil servants, ensuring a standardized and fair process. The Civil Servants Appointment, Promotion, and Transfer Rules, 1973, specifically govern the procedure for promotion and other matters for Grade 18 and above, providing a clear framework for career advancement.

The rules are a set of regulations that provide guidelines for the appointment, promotion, and transfer of civil servants, ensuring that all civil servants are subject to the same standards and procedures. The Civil Servants Appointment, Promotion, and Transfer Rules, 1973, are applicable to all civil servants, providing a comprehensive framework for managing their careers and ensuring that the government's human resources are utilized effectively. The rules are designed to promote fairness, transparency, and accountability in the appointment, promotion, and transfer of civil servants, which is essential for maintaining a competent and efficient public service.

The Civil Servants Appointment, Promotion, and Transfer Rules, 1973, have been established to provide a clear and consistent approach to managing the careers of civil servants, ensuring that all appointments, promotions, and transfers are made based on merit and in accordance with established procedures. By providing a standardized framework for appointment, promotion, and transfer, the rules help to promote a culture of professionalism and excellence within the public service, which is essential for delivering high-quality services to citizens. Overall, the Civil Servants Appointment, Promotion, and Transfer Rules, 1973, play a critical role in ensuring that the government's human resources are managed effectively and efficiently, which is essential for achieving its goals and objectives.

nodes.name: Ministry of Finance
nodes.description:
The Ministry of Finance is a government department that plays a crucial role in the country's financial decision-making process. It is responsible for overseeing the financial affairs of the country and is consulted for various financial matters, including post creation and abolition. The ministry is also involved in publishing important documents, such as the Economic Survey of Pakistan, which provides insights into the country's economic performance.

The Ministry of Finance is responsible for managing the country's finances, including approval of additional pay for current charge appointments and overseeing various occupational groups, such as the Accounts Group and the Income Tax Group. It is also responsible for financial management and policy, providing guidance and consultation to other government departments on financial matters. The ministry works closely with other government departments, including the Establishment Division, regarding matters such as TA admissibility.

In addition to its financial management responsibilities, the Ministry of Finance is also responsible for making policies for government servants, including re-employment and medical examination. The ministry issues office memorandums and other guidelines to ensure that financial matters are handled in a fair and transparent manner. Overall, the Ministry of Finance is a key government department that plays a vital role in the country's financial management and decision-making process. It provides financial guidance, oversight, and consultation to other government departments, and is responsible for ensuring that the country's financial affairs are managed effectively and efficiently.

nodes.name: BS-17
nodes.description:
BS-17 refers to a specific pay scale or rank within the government's civil service hierarchy. It is a grade level in the government's employment hierarchy, associated with certain positions such as Section Officer and Private Secretary. BS-17 is used as a reference point for rotation and promotion considerations for officers. It is also a pay scale designation for officers in the government's bureaucratic hierarchy.

The pay scale is used for government employees and is a part of the government's employee salary structure. BS-17 is also a rank or grade level for officers in the civil service, including PAS and PSP officers. It is a job grade in the Pakistani government's pay scale and is used to determine the salary and benefits of government employees. In general, BS-17 is a pay scale or grade level in the government's employment hierarchy, and it plays a crucial role in the government's human resource management.

Overall, BS-17 is an important concept in the government's pay scale and employment hierarchy, and it is used to classify and compensate government employees based on their rank, position, and level of responsibility. The pay scale is an essential part of the government's personnel management system, and it helps to ensure that government employees are fairly compensated and recognized for their contributions to the public service. BS-17 is a key component of this system, and it is used to support the recruitment, retention, and development of talented and dedicated government employees.

nodes.name: BS-21
nodes.description:
BS-21 refers to a specific pay scale designation within the government hierarchy, particularly in the context of the Pakistani government's pay scale. It is considered the highest mentioned pay scale or rank within the civil service, representing a significant level of career progression. The selection to this scale is based on specific criteria, which may include meritorious service. Officers can be granted this pay scale on the basis of meeting these criteria, which may also involve conditions such as transfer out of a government position after a certain number of years, typically 10 years.

BS-21 is a basic scale pay grade in the government service hierarchy, surpassing the BS-20 grade. It is a grade or rank in the civil service hierarchy, indicating a high level of seniority and responsibility. The pay scale is used to determine the compensation and benefits for officers holding positions at this level. As the highest pay scale, BS-21 is a coveted rank that officers strive to achieve through dedication and exceptional performance.

In summary, BS-21 is a prestigious pay scale designation that signifies a high level of achievement and seniority within the government hierarchy. It is a rank that is granted to officers based on specific criteria, including meritorious service, and is considered the pinnacle of career progression in the civil service.

nodes.name: CSB
nodes.description:
The Central Selection Board (CSB) is an organization responsible for various aspects of civil servant management. The CSB is involved in the promotion process of civil servants, where it considers promotions to higher posts. This is achieved through evaluating and selecting civil servants for promotion, as well as reviewing proposals for promotions submitted by ministries and departments. The CSB also provides recommendations for induction and awards marks as part of its evaluation process.

In addition to its role in promotions, the CSB is also responsible for evaluating and selecting employees. This includes considering the suitability of individuals for certain positions and making recommendations accordingly. The CSB's role in employee evaluation and selection highlights its importance in ensuring that civil servants are appropriately qualified and experienced for their roles.

The Central Selection Board operates as a committee or board, with a specific mandate to manage the promotion and evaluation process. Its involvement in the promotion process of government servants underscores its significance in the public sector. While the specific details of the CSB's operations may vary, its core function of evaluating and recommending individuals for promotions and positions remains a crucial aspect of its work.

Overall, the Central Selection Board plays a vital role in the management of civil servants, focusing on promotions, evaluations, and selections. Its work is critical to ensuring that civil servants are properly qualified and experienced for their roles, and that the promotion process is fair and effective.

nodes.name: DSB
nodes.description:
The Departmental Selection Board (DSB) is a committee responsible for considering promotions of civil servants to higher posts within their respective departments. The DSB plays a crucial role in the promotion process, where it evaluates and selects civil servants for promotion. This body is involved in reviewing proposals for promotions submitted by ministries and departments, and it provides recommendations for promotions. The DSB is also responsible for awarding marks to candidates, which helps in determining their suitability for promotion.

The Departmental Selection Board is a key component in the career advancement of civil servants, and its primary function is to ensure that promotions are made based on merit and fairness. The DSB's evaluation process involves assessing the performance and qualifications of civil servants, and it provides a platform for departments to identify and recognize talented individuals who are deserving of promotion. By following established guidelines for civil servant promotion, the DSB helps to maintain the integrity and transparency of the promotion process.

As an organization, the DSB operates within the framework of governmental regulations and guidelines, and its decisions have a significant impact on the careers of civil servants. The DSB's role is not limited to promotion; it is also involved in the evaluation process, where it assesses the performance of civil servants and provides feedback for improvement. Overall, the Departmental Selection Board (DSB) is a vital institution that contributes to the development and growth of the civil service, and its work is essential for ensuring that the most capable and deserving individuals are promoted to higher positions.

nodes.name: DPC
nodes.description:
The Departmental Promotion Committee (DPC) is a committee responsible for considering promotions of civil servants to higher posts within their respective departments. The DPC plays a crucial role in the promotion process of civil servants, evaluating and selecting candidates for promotion within their departments. This committee is involved in the evaluation and selection process, reviewing proposals for promotions submitted by ministries and departments.

The DPC is also responsible for evaluating candidates for promotions, ensuring that the promotion process is fair and based on merit. As an organization, the DPC is concerned with guidelines for civil servant promotion, providing a framework for departments to follow when considering promotions. The committee's responsibilities include evaluations of civil servants, assessing their performance and potential for advancement.

In essence, the Departmental Promotion Committee is a body that oversees the promotion process of government servants, ensuring that promotions are based on merit and following established guidelines. The DPC's role is critical in maintaining a fair and transparent promotion process, and its evaluations and selections have a significant impact on the careers of civil servants. Overall, the DPC is a key component of the civil service system, responsible for identifying and promoting talented and deserving candidates to higher posts.

nodes.name: Government Servants (Conduct) Rules
nodes.description:
The Government Servants (Conduct) Rules are regulations that govern the behavior and conduct of government servants. These rules are a set of regulations that outline the expected behavior and conduct of government servants, providing a code of conduct that guides their actions. The Government Servants (Conduct) Rules, 1964, are a specific set of rules that regulate the conduct of government servants, including provisions related to engaging in private business, trade, and consultancy work.

The rules are designed to ensure that government servants behave in a manner that is ethical and professional, and that their conduct does not compromise their official duties or bring discredit to the government. The regulations cover various aspects of a government servant's behavior, including their interactions with the public, their use of official resources, and their involvement in outside activities. The Government Servants (Conduct) Rules are an important part of the administrative framework, as they help to maintain the integrity and credibility of the government and its servants.

Overall, the Government Servants (Conduct) Rules are a crucial component of the governance structure, providing a clear framework for government servants to follow in their official and personal lives. By outlining the expected standards of behavior and conduct, the rules help to promote a culture of accountability, transparency, and integrity within the government, and ensure that government servants are able to perform their duties in a fair, impartial, and efficient manner.

nodes.name: Estt. Division
nodes.description:
The Estt. Division, also known as the Establishment Division, is a government organization responsible for a wide range of administrative and regulatory functions. Its primary role involves issuing office memoranda, orders, and guidelines related to government services, personnel management, and employee benefits. The division is tasked with establishing and implementing policies, rules, and regulations for government servants, including those related to appointments, re-employment, deputation, and retirement.

The Estt. Division plays a crucial role in overseeing administrative control, managing government services, and providing support for the confirmation process. It is also responsible for designing proformas for the appointment of consultants and issuing circulars and orders related to employment and re-employment policies. Additionally, the division deals with the administration of government servants, including service counting and promotions. The Estt. Division has issued various office memorandums, including one regarding the repeal of the Removal from Service (Special Powers) Ordinance, 2000.

The division's responsibilities encompass establishing and managing government policies and procedures, as well as issuing notifications and orders. It is also involved in making decisions and providing guidelines for personnel management, including rules and regulations for deputation and employment. The Estt. Division's role is essential in ensuring the smooth functioning of government services and the effective management of government servants. Overall, the Estt. Division is a vital component of the government's administrative framework, responsible for a broad range of functions that support the efficient operation of government services and the well-being of government servants.

nodes.name: Finance Secretary
nodes.description: The Finance Secretary is responsible for resolving major difficulties that may arise during the implementation of instructions.<SEP>Finance Secretary is a government official responsible for resolving difficulties in implementing decisions.<SEP>The Finance Secretary has approval authority for certain appointments in BPS 19 or equivalent.

nodes.name: Ministry Of Finance
nodes.description:
The Ministry of Finance is a government ministry responsible for overseeing financial and economic matters. It is involved in the approval of schemes for departmental examinations and has oversight of the Federal Board of Revenue. The Ministry of Finance plays a crucial role in managing the country's finances, including salaries of Government servants, and provides financial sanctions for recruitment. It is also responsible for issuing office memoranda and providing financial guidance on various matters, including the creation and utilization of Leave Reserve positions.

The Ministry of Finance has a representative in the Council on Rights of Persons with Disabilities and is consulted by the Estt. Division for personnel matters. It is involved in decision-making and consultation regarding financial matters, including those related to government servants. The Ministry has considered the operation of the 'next below rule' in government offices and has issued office memoranda regarding seniority and deputation rules. It is also responsible for informing government servants about discussing official matters with outsiders.

The Ministry of Finance concurs with the Estt. Division on certain office memoranda and is involved in financial decision-making. It oversees financial matters, including those related to government servants, and is responsible for approving the scheme for the departmental examination for accelerated promotion in Grade 17. The Ministry is one of the ministries requested to bring certain matters to the notice of government servants. Overall, the Ministry of Finance plays a vital role in the country's financial management and decision-making process.

nodes.name: Intelligence Bureau
nodes.description:
The Intelligence Bureau is a government agency responsible for intelligence gathering and national security. It is a department that deals with the collection and analysis of intelligence, playing a crucial role in ensuring the safety and security of the nation. As part of its responsibilities, the Intelligence Bureau conducts security clearance and verifies the antecedents of government employees, providing assessments and evaluations of candidates for various positions.

The Intelligence Bureau is also involved in the process of clearing government personnel before they proceed abroad on government appointments or deputation, highlighting its role in maintaining national security and protecting sensitive information. The agency employs section officers and is one of the specified ministries or divisions where eligible candidates can serve or be re-employed, including armed forces officers. Additionally, the Intelligence Bureau provides political record checks for new employees, further emphasizing its focus on security and verification.

In summary, the Intelligence Bureau is a multifaceted organization that handles a range of tasks, from intelligence gathering and analysis to security clearance and personnel evaluation, making it a vital component of the government's national security framework. Its responsibilities encompass various aspects of security, personnel verification, and national protection, underscoring its significance in maintaining the country's safety and integrity.

nodes.name: Superannuation
nodes.description:
Superannuation refers to the age or point at which a person, typically a government servant or civil servant, retires from their job, marking the end of their service period. This concept is closely associated with the rules and regulations governing the retirement process for government servants. The age of superannuation is often specified, commonly at 60 years, after which the individual is eligible for a pension and other retirement benefits.

The descriptions of superannuation emphasize its relation to government service, highlighting the eligibility of government servants for retirement and the subsequent receipt of a pension. It is essential to note that superannuation is not only about reaching a certain age but also about the status of being retired from government service. The process of superannuation is subject to specific regulations, which are designed to govern the transition of government servants into retirement.

In summary, superannuation is a concept that encompasses both the age at which government servants are eligible to retire and the process of retirement itself, including the entitlement to a pension and other benefits. The emphasis on government service and the structured nature of the retirement process underscore the importance of understanding superannuation in the context of public sector employment.

nodes.name: National Accountability Bureau
nodes.description:
The National Accountability Bureau is a government agency responsible for overseeing accountability and anti-corruption efforts in Pakistan. It is an organization that takes actions against government servants who have committed crimes, and is involved in inquiries and investigations against civil servants. The National Accountability Bureau is responsible for investigating and prosecuting cases of corruption and other crimes, and is a key entity in the process of accountability and investigation.

The National Accountability Bureau is a regulatory body that oversees accountability and anti-corruption efforts, and is responsible for implementing ordinances and laws related to accountability and corruption. It conducts inquiries and investigations into offenses under its ordinance, and is responsible for nominating members to the Conciliation Committee and overseeing the accountability process. The National Accountability Bureau is also responsible for investigating and prosecuting cases of corruption and financial irregularities, and is a government agency that combats corruption and corrupt practices.

The National Accountability Bureau's primary goal is to eradicate corruption and hold accountable those accused of corrupt practices. It is a constitutional body established for the whole of Pakistan, and is responsible for ensuring accountability in the country. The National Accountability Bureau plays a crucial role in investigating and prosecuting cases of corruption, and is an investigating agency that deals with corruption cases. Overall, the National Accountability Bureau is a key organization involved in the process of accountability and anti-corruption efforts in Pakistan.

nodes.name: Pension
nodes.description:
Pension refers to a type of retirement benefit or financial support provided to individuals, particularly government servants, after their retirement or death. This benefit is intended to support the livelihood of the retiree or their family members. The pension is typically calculated based on the individual's salary and years of service, and may be paid as a regular monthly payment.

The term "pension" encompasses various types of benefits, including those paid to government employees, armed forces personnel, and civil servants. In some cases, pensions are also provided to the families of deceased government employees, including spouses and children. The specific regulations and calculations governing pension payments vary depending on the relevant laws and policies, such as the Civil Servants Act and the Estacode.

Pensions can take various forms, including monthly payments, series of payments, or lump-sum benefits. They are designed to provide financial security and support to individuals after their retirement, and may be subject to certain conditions and eligibility criteria. Overall, the concept of pension refers to a broad range of retirement benefits and financial support systems aimed at ensuring the well-being of government servants and their families after retirement or in the event of death.

nodes.name: Cabinet Division
nodes.description:
The Cabinet Division is a government department responsible for overseeing a wide range of cabinet-related matters, national institutions, and regulatory bodies. It plays a crucial role in coordinating and implementing government policies, as well as issuing guidelines, notifications, and directives. The Cabinet Division is involved in high-level decision-making and committee formations, and it provides support and guidance on policy matters to various government departments and ministries.

The Cabinet Division is also responsible for managing the Toshakhana, which involves organizing the sales of gifts received by government functionaries, and overseeing the implementation of rules and regulations related to these gifts. Additionally, it participates in the selection and appointment process of staff in the National School Of Public Policy. The department is also tasked with bringing certain matters to the notice of government servants and issuing directives and guidelines for government officers and ministries.

The Cabinet Division has issued various circulars and orders, including one regarding the use of the term "competent authority" and another regarding the discussion of official matters with outsiders. It also prepares security instructions and is responsible for coordinating and implementing government policies. The department supports the cabinet in its functions and is involved in processing cases for approval by the Prime Minister. Overall, the Cabinet Division plays a vital role in the functioning of the government, ensuring that policies are implemented effectively and that the cabinet is supported in its decision-making processes.

nodes.name: Selection Board
nodes.description:
The Selection Board is an entity responsible for making various selections and recommendations for posts within governmental and organizational structures. Its scope includes evaluating and selecting candidates for consultant positions, appointments in autonomous bodies, and government positions, including civil posts and contract appointments. The Selection Board is also involved in the appointment of officers to certain posts and is responsible for evaluating civil servants for promotion.

The Selection Board is headed by the Chairman of the Federal Public Service Commission in some cases, and it is also constituted by the Prime Minister for the purpose of selection for promotion or transfer to posts in basic pay scales 19 to 21 and equivalent. The board is responsible for recommending a panel of candidates for various positions and compensation packages. It considers and recommends candidates for appointments in autonomous bodies and certain other appointments. The Selection Board operates under the purview of the Federal Government, evaluating candidates for government positions and promotions, and its recommendations are crucial for the selection process.

While the descriptions provided highlight various roles and responsibilities of the Selection Board, it is evident that the board plays a vital role in the selection and appointment process for different governmental and organizational positions. The Selection Board's responsibilities include evaluating candidates, making recommendations, and selecting candidates for various positions, demonstrating its importance in the human resource management and development of governmental and organizational structures. The board's composition and responsibilities may vary depending on the specific context and the entity it serves, but its primary function remains the evaluation and selection of candidates for various positions.

nodes.name: BPS 20
nodes.description:
BPS 20 is a pay scale or grade level used within the government's employment hierarchy. It is utilized to determine the seniority of high-ranking officers, including Joint Secretaries. This pay scale is considered a higher level for civil servants, with specific requirements for promotion, such as completion of 17 years of service in BPS 17 and above for acting charge appointments.

BPS 20 is situated within the government's pay structure, below BPS 21, and is applicable to government servants and employees. It indicates the level of seniority and compensation for civil servants, reflecting their position within the government's employment hierarchy. The pay scale is an integral part of the government's employment system, providing a clear framework for career progression and remuneration.

Overall, BPS 20 plays a significant role in the government's pay structure, serving as a benchmark for seniority, promotion, and compensation for high-ranking officers and civil servants. Its specific requirements and position within the hierarchy underscore its importance in maintaining a structured and equitable employment system.

nodes.name: Promotion
nodes.description:
Promotion refers to the process of advancing an individual to a higher position or rank within an organization. This can occur in various contexts, including government service, where a civil servant or government servant is promoted to a higher position or rank. In a similar vein, officers can be promoted to higher grades or positions, indicating a progression in their career based on performance and other factors.

The concept of promotion is not limited to government or military contexts but is also prevalent in private organizations, where employees can be promoted to higher positions or grades based on their performance and other considerations. This advancement signifies a recognition of the individual's capabilities, contributions, and potential for further growth within the organization. Whether in the public or private sector, promotion is an important aspect of career development, reflecting an organization's investment in its personnel and a means of rewarding and motivating high-performing individuals.

Across different descriptions, the core idea of promotion remains consistent: it involves the movement of an individual to a higher level of responsibility, rank, or position. While the specific context may vary, the underlying principle of recognizing and rewarding merit and potential for further contribution remains a common thread. Regardless of whether it pertains to civil servants, officers, or employees in general, promotion serves as a crucial mechanism for talent retention, career progression, and organizational growth.

nodes.name: Section Officers
nodes.description:
Section Officers are government servants who hold specific administrative positions within the government bureaucracy. They can be promoted to Grade 17 and work at Headquarters, and are part of the Office Management Group in the Federal Secretariat. These officers are civil servants who can be recruited through direct recruitment or promotion, and were eligible for regular appointment under the former CSS Rules. They are appointed through competitive examinations conducted by the Federal Public Service Commission.

Section Officers are responsible for managing specific sections within the Federal Government, and are administrative staff who oversee various departments and services. They work in different ministries and divisions, and some are appointed as Leave Reserve Officers to handle specific tasks. As government employees, they are part of the Leave Reserve and Training Reserve, and are subject to rules and guidelines set by Estt. Division regarding their application for higher posts and transfer.

These officers are employed in specific posts under the Section Officers' Scheme and are eligible for promotion and transfer. They are government officials responsible for specific sections or departments within a government institution, and are a class of officers working in various departments and services. Section Officers are government employees who work in various Ministries/Divisions, and are responsible for managing and overseeing specific sections or departments within an organization. They are a type of government employee holding specific administrative positions, and play a crucial role in the government bureaucracy.

nodes.name: National Assembly
nodes.description:
The National Assembly is the legislative body of Pakistan, serving as the lower house of the country's legislative branch. It is comprised of elected representatives and plays a crucial role in lawmaking and oversight within the government. The National Assembly has various responsibilities, including the appointment of the Chairman of the National Accountability Bureau. Additionally, it receives reports from the Federal Public Service Commission and has representatives on the Council on Rights of Persons with Disabilities, as well as other councils.

The National Assembly has experienced periods of suspension, notably in pursuance of the Proclamation of Emergency and the Proclamation of October 14, 1999. Despite these suspensions, the legislative body remains a vital component of Pakistan's government, responsible for representing the interests of its citizens. It is worth noting that government servants are not allowed to approach the National Assembly for personal favors, emphasizing the body's commitment to upholding the law and maintaining its integrity.

As a legislative body, the National Assembly is involved in various activities, including lawmaking and oversight. Its representatives are elected to serve the people, and the body works to ensure that the rights of all citizens, including persons with disabilities, are protected and represented. While the National Assembly has faced challenges and suspensions, it remains a fundamental institution in Pakistan's government, working to promote the well-being and prosperity of the nation.

nodes.name: Islamabad
nodes.description:
Islamabad is the capital city of Pakistan. It is notable for being the location of several important government institutions, including the Federal Public Service Commission (FPSC) headquarters. The FPSC is responsible for overseeing public service examinations, which are indeed held in Islamabad. Additionally, the city is home to the Pakistan Public Administration Research Centre and serves as the permanent seat of the Service Tribunal.

As the capital, Islamabad hosts various government offices and is a key location for administrative activities. It has been mentioned as an example of a place where government vacancies may occur. Furthermore, the city is subject to certain regulations, such as the prohibition on government servants attending drinking parties. Islamabad's role as a major administrative hub is further emphasized by its being a possible exam center for various national-level examinations.

The presence of the FPSC and other government institutions in Islamabad underscores the city's significance in Pakistan's administrative framework. The city's position as the capital and its hosting of important government offices and examinations make it a central location for the country's public service activities. Overall, Islamabad's status as the capital city of Pakistan is marked by its importance in the country's administrative and governmental functions.

nodes.name: Pakistan Audit and Accounts Service

The Pakistan Audit and Accounts Service is a government service responsible for auditing and accounting functions. It is an occupational group that comprises all posts in the Pakistan Audit and Accounts Department, Military Accounts Department, and Railway Accounts Department. Previously known as the Accounts Group, this service is a department within the government, operating under the Ministry of Finance and the Auditor General.

The Pakistan Audit and Accounts Service is a key component of the Finance Services, responsible for providing audit and accounts services to the government. It is one of the occupational groups and services that candidates can apply for, indicating its importance in the country's administrative structure. As a government agency, the Pakistan Audit and Accounts Service plays a crucial role in auditing and accounting for government funds, ensuring transparency and accountability in the management of public resources.

The service is part of the government's organizational framework, with its responsibilities and functions aligned with the broader objectives of the Ministry of Finance. The Pakistan Audit and Accounts Service is a vital institution in Pakistan's public sector, contributing to the effective management of the country's financial resources. Overall, the Pakistan Audit and Accounts Service is a government service that encompasses various departments and functions, all aimed at promoting good governance and fiscal discipline in the public sector.

nodes.name: Federal Secretariat
nodes.description:
The Federal Secretariat is a government institution in Pakistan responsible for administrative and managerial tasks. It serves as the administrative hub of the Federal Government, overseeing various departments and ministries. The Federal Secretariat is the location where the Office Management Group and the Secretariat Group operate, and it is also where section officers, who may be eligible for promotional examinations, are employed.

The Federal Secretariat is a central government's administrative body, responsible for overseeing various departments and attached offices. It is a government organization that employs officers of the Pakistan Administrative Service, and it is also a location where retired officers can be appointed as Section Officers. The institution plays a crucial role in the federal government, serving as its administrative headquarters and handling various administrative matters.

Despite its importance, the Federal Secretariat is currently facing challenges, including weak base, bad quality, and delivery gaps at the Section level. However, it remains a vital part of the government, responsible for overseeing various ministries and divisions. The Federal Secretariat is also a government building where federal authorities are based, and it is a department within the Federal Government that oversees various administrative tasks.

Overall, the Federal Secretariat is a key government institution in Pakistan, responsible for administrative and managerial tasks, and it plays a vital role in the federal government's operations. It is a government body that oversees attached departments and subordinate offices, and it is a location where eligible candidates can serve to meet the condition for promotional examinations. The Federal Secretariat is a crucial part of the government's administrative structure, and it continues to play an important role in the country's governance.

Within the government, a Section Officer can be found in various departments, including the OMG, where the position is categorized into two grades: BS-17 and BS-18.

nodes.name: Deputy Secretary
nodes.description:
The Deputy Secretary is a high-ranking government official responsible for various administrative tasks. This position is found within the federal or provincial governments and is part of the government hierarchy. A Deputy Secretary is typically involved in administrative and managerial tasks, serving as a stepping stone for promotion to Joint Secretary. They work under the Joint Secretary and assist in administrative tasks, overseeing various administrative functions, and are responsible for signing and approving requisitions.

The Deputy Secretary is a senior official in the government, responsible for overseeing administrative tasks, including the forwarding of applications. This position can be filled through a written test and interview, promotion, horizontal movement, or direct appointment. Officers of the Inland Revenue Service, as well as other groups, may be eligible for this post based on selection. The Deputy Secretary is also an official who may respond to queries from the Establishment Division and is designated to receive representations from recognized service associations.

In the context of the government, the Deputy Secretary is a bureaucratic position within the Secretariat posts, below the Joint Secretary in rank. This position is a mid-level government position in the secretariat, but also considered a high-ranking government official with significant administrative responsibilities. The Deputy Secretary assists the Secretary in managing administrative tasks and services, and is involved in decision-making. Overall, the Deputy Secretary plays a crucial role in the government, responsible for various administrative tasks and overseeing the implementation of government policies.
